title: GPU support goes wrong when the graphics card drivers update on the base system
body:
### Issue Description

Base System: Ubuntu 25.04
Container 1 runs: ComfyUI
Container 2 runs: Ollama
Container 3 runs: Open WebUI
Graphics card: NVidia GeForce RTX 3050
Podman: version 5.0.3
Podman Desktop: version 1.17

When the base system does an update of the graphics card then the applications inside the containers can't use the GPU.  My Ollama container doesn't require the GPU although it'll run faster with it, but the ComfyUI and the Open WebUI require the GPU otherwise they simply don't work.

I know that if I update the graphics card drivers with a combination of these commands on the base system.
```
sudo ubuntu-drivers list --gpgpu
cat /proc/driver/nvidia/version
sudo apt install nvidia-utils-570-server
sudo ubuntu-drivers install --gpgpu nvidia:570-server
```

Followed by the command.
```
nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output=/etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml
```

...then things will start working again after I've fixed it.  However I don't want to always have to fix Podman everytime I do `sudo apt upgrate` on the base system because that is just really annoying, it should just continue to work.  Can you fix Podman so that it doesn't go wrong and applications requiring the GPU will continue to work.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. sudo apt update
2. sudo apt upgrade
3.


### Describe the results you received

crun: cannot stat `/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/nvidia_icd.json`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the containers to run when I click the run button.

### podman info output

```yaml
Base System: Ubuntu 25.04
Container 1 runs: ComfyUI
Container 2 runs: Ollama
Container 3 runs: Open WebUI
Graphics card: NVidia GeForce RTX 3050
Podman: version 5.0.3
Podman Desktop: version 1.17
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Base System: Ubuntu 25.04
Container 1 runs: ComfyUI
Container 2 runs: Ollama
Container 3 runs: Open WebUI
Graphics card: NVidia GeForce RTX 3050
Podman: version 5.0.3
Podman Desktop: version 1.17

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
can you try with mainline or at the very minimum version 5.5 ? 

i'm not dead sure we can do much with this because the system is changing things under our feet.  it might help to identify what exactly is happening with the upgrade to see if mitigation is even possible.
I think Nvidia need to be asked if this is supposed to work, or if this kind of update should mandate a container restart.

This is up to nvidia not us, podman mounts whatever the cdi spec file says. If the paths on the host change after an update it is normal that the cdi config must be changed to use the new paths. That is even documented by nvidia here:
https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/container-toolkit/latest/cdi-support.html
> You must generate a new CDI specification after any of the following changes:
>
>    You change the device or CUDA driver configuration.
>
>    You use a location such as /var/run/cdi that is cleared on boot.
>
> A configuration change can occur when MIG devices are created or removed, or when the driver is upgraded.

Now logically the easiest way to solve this for a user is to have some kind of hook that regenerates the config when the driver is updated.

Or ask nvidia to fix their drivers to nor require path changes on every update.

> can you try with mainline or at the very minimum version 5.5 ?
> 
> i'm not dead sure we can do much with this because the system is changing things under our feet. it might help to identify what exactly is happening with the upgrade to see if mitigation is even possible.

The default repositories that come with Ubuntu 25.04 seem to have Podman 5.0.3

Do you know of any repository that I can add to my Ubuntu base system where they have a more recent version of Podman like 5.5?

I've tried all sorts from duckduckgo and found nothing except ...
```
Description:
DEPRECATED!!!!!
```

I want to drive this to root cause before closing.  I've simply reopened to keep it on my radar.  Will close in future.  If not closed in 30 days (or progress has been reported), let's close it again assuming no interest.

This is expected behavior with how things work today in NVIDIA Container Toolkit.

The CDI spec file that container toolkit generates (e.g. `/etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml`) contains references to files and resources that may change during a GPU driver update. When these things change you *must* regenerate the spec file with the new values for CDI to work as expected.

As @matthewbaynham points out, re-running `nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output=/etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml` makes things start working (again, as expected).

A simple improvement I like to do is to add something like this to your root crontab:

```
@reboot nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output /etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml
```

Then on every reboot (which is generally recommended after a driver update) you'll always get the correct CDI spec file. It is totally safe to re-generate the file whenever.
